content:
# Joshua Chapter 13 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 236-237 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_013_page_001.jpg`

1. Now Joshua was old, advanced in days, and YHWH said to him: "You are old, advanced in days, and very much of the land remains to be possessed.

2. This is the land that remains: all the regions of the Philistines and all the Geshurites,

3. from the Shihor which is before Egypt, and as far as the border of Ekron northward — it is reckoned to the Canaanites — the five lords of the Philistines: the Gazite, the Ashdodite, the Ashkelonite, the Gittite, and the Ekronite; also the Avvim

4. from the south; all the land of the Canaanites, and Mearah that belongs to the Sidonians, as far as Aphek, as far as the border of the Amorites;

5. and the land of the Gebalites, and all Lebanon toward the rising of the sun, from Baal-gad below Mount Hermon as far as Lebo-hamath;

6. all the inhabitants of the hill country from Lebanon as far as Misrephoth-maim, all the Sidonians — I myself will drive them out from before the Sons of Israel. Only allot it to Israel as an inheritance, just as I have commanded you.

7. Now therefore divide this land as an inheritance to the nine tribes and the half-tribe of Manasseh."

8. With the other half-tribe, the Reubenites and the Gadites received their inheritance, which Moses gave to them beyond the Jordan eastward, just as Moses the servant of YHWH gave to them:

9. from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and the city that is in the middle of the wadi, and all the tableland of Medeba as far as Dibon;

10. and all the cities of Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, as far as the border of the Sons of Ammon;

11. and Gilead, and the territory of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan as far as Salecah;

12. all the kingdom of Og in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei — he alone remained of the remnant of the Rephaim — and Moses struck them and dispossessed them.

13. But the Sons of Israel did not dispossess the Geshurites or the Maacathites, and Geshur and Maacath dwell in the midst of Israel to this day.

14. Only to the tribe of Levi he gave no inheritance; the fire offerings of YHWH, the God of Israel, are its inheritance, just as he spoke to them.

15. And Moses gave to the tribe of the Sons of Reuben according to their clans.

16. And their territory was from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and the city that is in the middle of the wadi, and all the tableland by Medeba;

17. Heshbon and all its cities that are on the tableland: Dibon, and Bamoth-baal, and Beth-baal-meon,

18. and Jahaz, and Kedemoth, and Mephaath,

19. and Kiriathaim, and Sibmah, and Zereth-shahar on the hill of the valley,

20. and Beth-peor, and the slopes of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth,

21. and all the cities of the tableland, and all the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, whom Moses struck along with the leaders of Midian: Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba — the princes of Sihon, inhabitants of the land.

22. And Balaam the son of Beor, the diviner, the Sons of Israel killed with the sword among their slain.

23. And the border of the Sons of Reuben was the Jordan and its bank. This was the inheritance of the Sons of Reuben according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

24. And Moses gave to the tribe of Gad, to the Sons of Gad, according to their clans.

25. And their territory was Jazer, and all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the Sons of Ammon, as far as Aroer which is before Rabbah;

26. and from Heshbon as far as Ramath-mizpeh and Betonim, and from Mahanaim as far as the border of Debir;

27. and in the valley: Beth-haram, and Beth-nimrah, and Succoth, and Zaphon — the rest of the kingdom of Sihon king of Heshbon — the Jordan and its bank, as far as the lower end of the Sea of Chinnereth, beyond the Jordan eastward.

28. This is the inheritance of the Sons of Gad according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

29. And Moses gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh, and it was for the half-tribe of the Sons of Manasseh according to their clans.

30. And their territory was from Mahanaim: all Bashan, all the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, and all the villages of Jair that are in Bashan — sixty cities;

31. and half of Gilead, and Ashtaroth, and Edrei, the cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan — these were for the Sons of Machir the son of Manasseh, for half of the Sons of Machir according to their clans.

32. These are what Moses allotted as inheritance in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan east of Jericho, eastward.

33. But to the tribe of Levi, Moses gave no inheritance; YHWH, the God of Israel — he is their inheritance, just as he spoke to them.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **YHWH (יהוה):** Rendered consistently as "YHWH" throughout, preserving the Tetragrammaton as it appears in the Leningrad Codex. Occurrences at vv. 1, 6, 8, 14, 33.

2. **"Sons of Israel" (בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל):** Retained literally per LOGOS convention (vv. 6, 13, 22). Similarly "Sons of Reuben" (בְּנֵי רְאוּבֵן, vv. 15, 23), "Sons of Gad" (בְּנֵי גָד, vv. 24, 28), "Sons of Ammon" (בְּנֵי עַמּוֹן, vv. 10, 25), "Sons of Machir" (בְּנֵי מָכִיר, v. 31), and "Sons of Manasseh" (בְּנֵי מְנַשֶּׁה, v. 29).

3. **"fire offerings" (אִשֵּׁי, v. 14):** Hebrew *isheh* — traditionally rendered "offerings made by fire." The term's etymology is debated: it may derive from *esh* ("fire") or from an unrelated root meaning "gift/food-offering." The LXX Vaticanus reads θυσίαι ("sacrifices"), broadening the term. LOGOS retains the connection to fire as the MT vocalizes it, rendering "fire offerings" to preserve the Hebrew specificity without committing to a single disputed etymology.

4. **"YHWH, the God of Israel — he is their inheritance" (v. 33):** The MT of v. 33 differs from v. 14. In v. 14, the Levites' inheritance is described as "the fire offerings of YHWH" (אִשֵּׁי יהוה), whereas v. 33 states that "YHWH, the God of Israel — he is their inheritance" (יהוה אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל הוּא נַחֲלָתָם). This is not a contradiction but a progressive theological statement: the offerings are the practical provision, while YHWH himself is the ultimate inheritance. Both verses are rendered as the MT reads them.

5. **"the Rephaim" (הָרְפָאִים, v. 12):** Retained as a proper noun rather than translating as "giants" or "shades." The term designates an ancient population of unusual stature in the Transjordan region. The LXX renders γιγάντων ("giants"), but the Hebrew functions as an ethnic name.

6. **"lords" (סַרְנֵי, sarnei, v. 3):** The Hebrew *seren* (plural *seranim*) is a title unique to the Philistine rulers, distinct from the more common *melek* ("king") or *sar* ("prince/official"). Some scholars connect it to Greek *tyrannos* via Aegean origins. "Lords" is chosen as the most natural English equivalent for this distinctive Philistine political title, distinguishing it from *nesi'ei* ("princes") used for the Midianite leaders in v. 21.

7. **"Lebo-hamath" (לְבוֹא חֲמָת, v. 5):** This could be translated "the entrance of Hamath" (as a geographical description) or retained as the proper name "Lebo-hamath" (a specific place). Modern scholarship and archaeological evidence favor a specific location (modern Lebweh in Lebanon's Beqaa Valley). LOGOS retains "Lebo-hamath" as a place name, consistent with its identification as a fixed boundary marker throughout the Hebrew Bible (cf. Num 34:8; Judg 3:3; 1 Kgs 8:65).

8. **"Shihor" (שִׁיחוֹר, v. 3):** Likely the eastern branch of the Nile or a border stream near Egypt. The LXX reads "from the uninhabited region before Egypt" (ἀπὸ τῆς ἀοικήτου), interpreting rather than transliterating. LOGOS preserves the Hebrew proper noun.

9. **"tableland" (מִישׁוֹר, mishor, vv. 9, 16, 17, 21):** The Hebrew *mishor* denotes a level plateau, specifically the elevated plain east of the Dead Sea in Transjordan. "Tableland" preserves the topographical specificity better than the generic "plain" or "plateau."

10. **"diviner" (הַקּוֹסֵם, haqqosem, v. 22):** Balaam is explicitly called *qosem* ("diviner") here — a term consistently condemned in Israelite law (Deut 18:10). This is a significant characterization: while Numbers 22-24 presents Balaam's oracles as genuine prophecy from YHWH, Joshua 13:22 classifies his practice under forbidden divination. The formal equivalence preserves this tension without harmonization.

11. **"to this day" (עַד הַיּוֹם הַזֶּה, v. 13):** A formulaic phrase indicating the situation persisted at the time of the text's composition or final editing. This provides an important editorial marker for the literary history of Joshua.

12. **Verse structure of vv. 15-31:** The Transjordan allotments follow a formulaic pattern: (a) Moses gave to the tribe (vv. 15, 24, 29); (b) territorial description with boundary and city lists (vv. 16-22, 25-27, 30-31); (c) summary formula (vv. 23, 28). This tripartite structure is preserved in the translation through consistent rendering of the repeated Hebrew formulas.
